reasoning: Without web search capabilities, I am relying heavily on the provided market estimate of 0.09 from 10 days prior to the cutoff. The base rate for a UK Prime Minister resigning or being ousted in their first full calendar year following a landslide victory (170+ seat majority) is exceptionally low. Ousters typically require a highly coordinated internal rebellion or terminal scandals, neither of which are easy to achieve so early into a term, especially under current Labour party rules. The 9% implied probability likely encapsulates low-probability tail risks (severe health issues, unpredictable major scandals) and the typical slight overpricing of longshot events by prediction markets. With no specific evidence to indicate a major crisis between Oct 16 and Oct 26, I am adjusting slightly downward to account for longshot bias, submitting a final estimate of 8%.
